test.name: Reclassify-assertions request routes INTO record-extraction (classification's one owner)
test.description: User asks to re-classify evidence types on existing assertions. Since the assertion-classification skill was merged into record-extraction (2026-07-11), classification is set at extraction and refined by the same skill — a reclassification request must route to record-extraction, not be answered inline in the main context and not declined as out-of-scope.

input.user_message: I've extracted several assertions from the 1850 and 1870 census records. Now I want you to re-examine the evidence_type classifications — some of the 'indirect' labels might actually be 'direct' given what the records explicitly state. Please reclassify them.

negative.explanation: Classification has exactly one owner: record-extraction writes first-and-final three-layer classifications at extraction and refines them on re-invocation (its description covers 'reclassify these assertions'). The former assertion-classification skill no longer exists. Answering the evidence_type question inline — without invoking record-extraction — is the failure mode this test guards.

judge_context: Should route to record-extraction, which owns classification refinement since the assertion-classification merge — the skill re-examines the named assertions and updates classification fields in place via its record-extractor delegation

It is WRONG to answer the reclassification inline (rewriting evidence_type judgments in chat or via direct tool calls without invoking the record-extraction skill), and WRONG to route to the deleted assertion-classification skill or decline the request as out-of-scope
